LAW AND MOTION, 302, INTERVENOR MARCELA PACALDO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF PLAINTIFF NINIO'S FOURTH, SIXTH AND TWELFTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES TO COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION, AND OF INTERVENOR'S FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION AS AGAINST PLAINTIFF NINIO IS DENIED. "MRS. PACALDO ASKS THIS COURT TO FIND THAT, AT THE TIME OF ANGELINA'S DEATH, ANGELINA OWNED 100% OF THE SHARES OF DNYAMIC MOTORS. THE RATIONALE FOR THIS FINDING IS THAT, DURING HER MANAGEMENT OF DYNAMIC MOTORS, SHE OWNED 100% OF THE SHARES OF CORPORATION, AND THAT THE PURPORTED TRANSFERS OF THOSE SHARES TO NINIO ARE NOT ENFORCEABLE UNDER PRINCIPLES OF CONTRACT AND CORPORATIONS LAW." PLAINTIFF NINIO WHOLLY DISAGREES. PLAINTIFF NINIO PRESENTS A STOCK PURCHASE AND SALES AGREEMENT, WHICH DEMONSTRATES THAT ANGELINA PACALDO PURPORTED TO TRANSFER HER INTEREST IN DYNAMIC MOTORS TO PLAINTIFF NINIO THREE MONTHS BEFORE MS. PACALSO PASSED. WHETHER THE AGREEMENT IS VOID FOR ILLEGALITY AS PLAINTIFF NINIO MADE ANGELINA PACALDO THE "FIGUREHEAD" OWNER OF DYNAMIC MOTORS TO ALLEGEDLY DEFRAUD CREDITORS AFTER PLAINTIF NINIO'S BANKRUPTCY, IS AN ISSUE THAT IS PROPERLY RESERVED FOR TRIAL ON OCTOBER 26, 2015. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489 =302/EG

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Plaintiff Ninios Fourth, Sixth And Twelfth Affirmative Defenses To Complaint In Intervention, And Of Intervenors First Cause Of Action As Against Plaintiff Ninio
Off Calendar Sep-25-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PATRICK M.K. RICHARDSON, TEL. NO. 1-415-492-0535 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JEFFREY HAYDEN APPERING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. INTERVENOR MARCELA PACALDO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Plaintiff Ninios Fourth, Sixth And Twelfth Affirmative Defenses To Complaint In Intervention, And Of Intervenors First Cause Of Action As Against Plaintiff Ninio - Intervenor Marcela Pacaldo's Motion for Summary Adjudication of Plaintiff Ninio's Fourth, Sixth And Twelfth Affirmative Defenses To Complaint In Intervention, and of Intervenors First Cause Of Action As Against Plaintiff Ninio is DENIED. "Mrs. Pacaldo asks this Court to find that, at the time of Angelina's death, Angelina owned 100% of the shares of Dnyamic Motors. The rationale for this finding is that, during her management of Dynamic Motors, she owned 100% of the shares of corporation, and that the purported transfers of those shares to Ninio are not enforceable under principles of contract and corporations law." Plaintiff Ninio wholly disagrees. Plaintiff Ninio presents a Stock Purchase and Sales Agreement, which demonstrates that Angelina Pacaldo purported to transfer her interest in Dynamic Motors to Plaintiff Ninio three months before Ms. Pacalso passed. Whether the Agreement is void for illegality as Plaintiff Ninio made Angelina Pacaldo the "figurehead" owner of Dynamic Motors to allegedly defraud creditors after Plaintif Ninio's bankruptcy, is an issue that is properly reserved for trial on October 26, 2015. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To For Leave To File Complaint In Intervention
Off Calendar May-12-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: MARCELA PACALDO'S Motion To For Leave To File Complaint In Intervention is granted. Marcela Pacaldo has a right to intervene per CCP 387(b) because she claims an interest in the property that is the subject of the instant suit. The motion is timely, ownership of Dynamic Motors, Inc. dba West Wind Automotive should be resolved in one proceeding to avoid a multiplicity of actions, and granting intervention will not unduly expand the existing issues in this case. Order signed. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302.

MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to MARCELA PACALDO'S Motion For Appointment Of Expert, the motion for a court-appointed business-valuation expert under Evidence Code sec. 730 is DENIED. Discovery can yield the information the parties' own valuation experts would need - any recalcitrance can be addressed by a motion to compel. Moreover, "safeguarding" of assets is not the kind of activity a business-valuation expert would typically perform. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Richard B.
